metadata.title: States PCS: Maharashtra GK Questions for MPSC Examinations

questions.question: ‘Shiv Bhojan’ scheme has launched by the government of which of the following states of India?
questions.explanation: ‘Shiv Bhojan’ scheme has launched by the government of Maharashtra. The objective of the scheme is to provide affordable & quality food to all, regardless of their caste, religion and financial status. A canteen named “Shiv Bhojan Thali” has inaugurated in Mumbai.

questions.question: The government of Maharashtra decided to rename the Maharashtra:”Smart gram yojana” after the name of who among the following personalities?
questions.explanation: The government of Maharashtra decided to rename Maharashtra: “Smart gram yojana” after the name of Raosaheb Ramrao Patil. He was a prominent member of the Nationalist Congress Party. He served the post of Home Minister of Maharashtra and Deputy Chief Minister of Maharashtra.

questions.question: Consider the following statements: Shri Jayatirtha, also known as Teekacharya, was a significant figure in the Dvaita school of Vedanta philosophy. He wrote important commentaries on the works of Madhvacharya. Shri Jayatirtha was born in Mangalwedha. He was associated with the revival of the Advaita tradition in the 14th century. Which of the above statements is / are correct?
questions.explanation: Statements 1, 2, and 3 are correct: Shri Jayatirtha was an important leader of the Dvaita school. He wrote authoritative commentaries on Madhvacharya’s works. Mangalwedha is his birthplace. Statement 4 is incorrect as he revived the Dvaita tradition, not Advaita. Jayatirtha’s work remains central to Dvaita followers.

questions.question: On which date was the Mumbai Metro’s Blue Line 1 officially inaugurated?
questions.explanation: Mumbai Metro Blue Line 1 was inaugurated on June 8, 2014. It is the first metro line in Mumbai and the fifth rapid transit system in India, serving the Mumbai Metropolitan Region, including cities such as Thane and Navi Mumbai.

questions.question: Ramchandrapur village, believed to be named after Ramchandra Yadava, was established by which dynasty’s prominent ruler?
questions.explanation: Ramchandra Yadava was a key ruler of the Seuna (Yadava) dynasty, noted for military success and cultural support, especially of Marathi literature. Ramchandrapur village is associated with his legacy in Maharashtra.

questions.question: At which fort in Maharashtra did the Maratha general Tanaji Malusare die during a historic battle in 1670?
questions.explanation: Tanaji Malusare died in 1670 fighting the Battle of Sinhagad against the Mughals. The fort, located near Pune, is renowned for this valiant Maratha victory and Tanaji’s heroism, which is celebrated in Maharashtra’s folklore and history.

questions.question: Who led the 1982 textile mill workers’ strike in Bombay?
questions.explanation: Dattatray Samant, a prominent trade union leader, led over 200,000 workers in the 1982 Bombay textile strike, one of India’s largest industrial actions. The strike lasted over a year and caused major mill closures, profoundly impacting Mumbai’s textile industry and urban landscape.

questions.question: Who was the first female Indian sociologist and anthropologist?
questions.explanation: Irawati Karve (1905-1970) pioneered Indian sociology and anthropology, founding the anthropology department at Poona University and contributing extensively to caste studies, kinship, and ethnology, especially in Maharashtra.

questions.question: The Mohini temple in Nevasa is dedicated to which deity?
questions.explanation: The Mohini temple in Nevasa, Maharashtra, is dedicated to Mohiniraj, a form of Vishnu. Built in 1773, it is maintained by the Bhojraj Pant Jahagirdar family. Archaeological studies reveal Nevasa’s historical depth, with evidence of habitation from the Paleolithic era through the Middle Ages.

questions.question: Which fort is famously called the “Jewel of the Sahyadris”?
questions.explanation: Ratangad Fort in Maharashtra, over 400 years old, is known as the “Jewel of the Sahyadris.” It features a natural rock cavity called ‘Nedhe’ and four historic gates. Located near Bhandardara, it was captured by Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj and is a famous trekking destination in Western Maharashtra.
